exemplar.title: The efficiency of the Overhand shuffle, Riffle shuffle, and Wash shuffle at randomizing a deck of cards

detailedExemplarData.title: The efficiency of the Overhand shuffle, Riffle shuffle, and Wash shuffle at randomizing a deck of cards

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical and coherent progression from introduction through methodology to conclusion
Clear use of headings, sub-headings, numbered figures and tables to structure the report
Mostly concise presentation with relevant content and minimal extraneous material
weaknesses: Repetitive reminders about the insufficiency of the mean in multiple sections
Formatting inconsistencies in table layouts and figure captions
Bibliography uses mixed citation formats and sometimes lacks details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Presentation were consistently assessed at band 3: the exploration is coherent and logically developed; organization is clear but with minor layout glitches; content is mostly concise though with some redundancy. These factors align with a 3/4 rating.

strengths: Standard statistical notation (xÌ, Ï, r) is used correctly throughout
Effective use of tables, photographs and verbal explanations to support findings
Good logical flow from data collection through computation to interpretation
weaknesses: Key symbols (e.g. fáµ¢, xáµ¢) are not defined in text
Contradictory statements about which shuffle has lower variability
Graphs and visual highlights of key results are missing, and one r value exceeds its valid range
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication shows mostly consistent use of language and notation (band 3), effective multiple representations (band 3), and good logical clarity (band 3). Occasional imprecision and lack of visual aids prevent a higher band, so a 3/4 is appropriate.

strengths: Designing and conducting original 20-trial experiments demonstrates clear independent initiative
Personal anecdotes (card games with friends) give genuine context
Extension to online shuffling shows creativity beyond routine laboratory work
weaknesses: Analysis relies on standard metrics; no novel randomness measure was developed
Approach, while personal, does not push into highly innovative territory
Mathematical depth of personal contribution remains conventional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of independent thinking, personal approach and initiative is significant but not outstanding, fitting band 2 across all strands. A score of 2/3 accurately reflects this level of personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on limitations of mean and Pearsonâs r
Thoughtful analysis of execution errors and small-sample effects
Clear evaluation of outcomes, recognizing human inconsistency and sample-size issues
weaknesses: Reflection is not sustained throughout the exploration
Future-oriented suggestions are mentioned only briefly
No justification or sample-size calculation is provided for trial design
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection demonstrates meaningful but not substantial depth (band 2), thoughtful analysis (band 2) and clear evaluation (band 2). The work stops short of comprehensive critical reflection, supporting a 2/3.

strengths: Relevant use of mean, standard deviation and Pearsonâs r linked clearly to the research question
Mathematics is commensurate with SL course level and applied effectively
Calculations are mostly correct and demonstrate thorough understanding of statistical measures
weaknesses: One reported r value lies outside its valid range, indicating a calculation error
Analysis is limited to three statistics; no additional measures (e.g., entropy, t-tests) were attempted
Some interpretation of standard deviation is oversimplified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and applied effectively at SL level (above basic competence) despite some errors in calculation and limited scope. These strengths and weaknesses align with a level above 3 but below expert mastery, justifying a 4/6 to balance the overall mark distribution.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations move logically from data collection to statistical calculation to interpretation, and the reader can see how conclusions arise. Occasional abrupt transitions and the unexplained appearance of an impossible r = â1.54 reduce overall clarity, keeping the work in the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ band.

The student demonstrates basic understanding of each statistic, but an r value of â1.54 reveals computational or conceptual error, and standard-deviation commentary occasionally misinterprets what constitutes good spread. These mistakes show only partial accuracy, fitting the descriptor âsome understanding with errorsâ.

The student employs tables for raw data, comparative tables for statistics, photographs of shuffles and brief verbal explanations. These multiple representations support understanding and are generally appropriate. Inclusion of proper graphs would have strengthened the presentation, so the work is judged âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction of motivation, description of methodology, data presentation, analysis and conclusion. Each section builds sensibly on the previous one and the reader can follow the argument without major difficulty. Although a few explanations are repetitive (e.g., repeated reminders that mean alone is insufficient), the overall logical progression is sound, placing the work firmly in the âcoherent and logically developedâ band rather than the âexcellentâ band which would demand tighter, highly polished progression throughout.

Collecting original data, comparing with an online algorithm and critiquing the measures used exhibit creativity and initiative above a routine treatment. However, methods and analysis remain fairly conventional and there is no sophisticated mathematical extension, placing the work comfortably in, but not above, band 2.

There is thoughtful examination of statistical results, recognition of limitations of Pearsonâs r, and discussion of skew from outliers. This shows analysis of both results and process, but the reasoning is not especially deep, and some errors (e.g. impossible r value) are unaddressed, keeping it at band 2.

The conclusion ranks shuffles and acknowledges limitations such as human inconsistency and small sample size, providing a clear if moderate evaluation. Future improvements are hinted at but not developed in detail, aligning with the descriptor of âclear evaluationâ rather than the comprehensive level.

The student reflects on why mean alone is insufficient, the effect of execution errors and trust issues in online shuffling. These comments go beyond mere description and constitute meaningful reflection, though they are brief and not integrated throughout, so the depth is not âsubstantialâ.

Standard statistical symbols (\(\overline{x},\;\sigma,\;r\)) are used correctly and formulas are written in mathematical notation. Language is generally appropriate, though a few imprecisions appear (e.g. calling the Pearson coefficient an âr-valueâ greater than 1). This satisfies âmostly consistentâ rather than âcompletely consistentâ use.

The personal contextâplaying cards with friendsâand the choice of variables and metrics demonstrate a genuine personal approach. The narrative frequently uses first-hand observations. Yet the design does not push into highly personal or innovative territory such as creating a new randomness metric, so the strand fits band 2.

Most of the 15 pages are devoted to describing the experiment, presenting results and interpreting them; there is little extraneous historical narrative and only occasional redundancy. Minor over-explanation of basic statistical ideas and duplicated comments about limitations keep it from the highest level of conciseness.

All mathematics used (mean, standard deviation, correlation coefficient) is relevant to addressing randomness and is applied in context. The student links each statistic to the research question, so usage is appropriate, though the range of techniques is limited.

Designing and running 20-trial experiments for three physical and one on-line shuffle shows clear independent initiative beyond textbook examples. Nevertheless, statistical tools and reasoning remain standard; there is no novel mathematical insight or model, so evidence of independent thinking is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered figures and tables give the report a recognisable structure with introduction-body-conclusion. Data, formulas and discussion are placed in appropriate sections. Nonetheless, some layout glitches (empty columns in tables, split sentences across pages) and a loosely attached bibliography prevent it from reaching the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard.

Mean, SD and Pearsonâs r are fully within the AA SL syllabus and are applied at the correct conceptual level. No higher-level mathematics is attempted, so the work is âcommensurate with course levelâ but does not warrant the higher bands demanding comprehensive or expert application.

annotations.comment: The extensive historical background on shuffling methods, while informative, distracts from the mathematical focus. Consider condensing this section to maintain a clear emphasis on the investigationâs math objectives.
The personal anecdote about the card game provides genuine context and demonstrates clear engagement with the topic from the outset.
The design uses seven shuffles per trial without justifying why 20 trials provide sufficient statistical reliability. Consider including a rationale or sample-size calculation.
Figure captions lack uniform formatting: âFigure 1. Riffle Shuffleâ appears as h2 while others use h4. Standardize caption styles for consistency.
The methodology references the GilbertâShannonâReeds modelâs seven-shuffle recommendation but does not critique its assumptions. Reflect on whether this model suits your trial conditions.
Only mean, standard deviation, and Pearsonâs r are used to assess randomness. Consider additional measures (e.g., clustering metrics or entropy) to deepen your analysis.
The Overhand Shuffle data table headings are misaligned and lack clear separation between trial number and card positions. Refine column structure for readability.
The placeholder header row in the Riffle Shuffle data table is empty, which may confuse readers. Remove or populate this row appropriately.
Column headings in the Riffle Shuffle table switch between bold and plain text. Apply a consistent style to distinguish headers from data.
In the standard deviation formula, the frequency symbol $f_i$ and variable $x_i$ are not defined in the text. Include definitions to ensure clarity of notation.
The interpretation suggests Overhand shuffleâs SD is best, conflicting with earlier statements praising the Wash shuffle. Align your comparative analysis to avoid contradiction.
The explanation of Pearsonâs r in the header ends abruptly (âif r has a positive value thenâ). Complete the sentence to fully describe the relationship interpretation.
The claim that online shuffling outperforms the Wash shuffle could be strengthened by a statistical test (e.g., comparing means via t-test) to verify significance.
The Pearsonâs r table includes an impossible value of $-1.54$, indicating a calculation error. Recompute this coefficient to fall within the valid range $[-1,1]$.
The LaTeX presentation of Pearsonâs formula is clear and accurate, effectively communicating the computation.
The discussion of Pearsonâs limitations shows thoughtful evaluation of method applicability, acknowledging potential pitfalls in linear analysis.
The rationale for combining mean, SD, and r in the analysis is coherent. Reinforce this by explicitly linking how each metric addresses fairness to the original research question.
The extension to online shuffling demonstrates originality and independent thinking in comparing physical and digital methods.
The online shuffle data table is comprehensive but could highlight key comparisons with summary rows or conditional formatting for faster interpretation.
The summary table of online results effectively consolidates metrics. Consider adding visual cues (e.g., bolding best values) to guide the reader to key findings.
The discussion of limitations notes small sample size but could propose specific follow-up trials or methods (e.g., automated shuffler) to improve reliability.
The conclusion recaps key findings but repeats earlier text. Streamline this section to avoid redundancy and focus on the studyâs implications.
Combining physical and online results in one paragraph makes it hard to delineate separate insights. Consider splitting for clearer comparative conclusions.
Bibliography entries use mixed citation formats and inconsistent punctuation. Adopt a single style guide (e.g., APA or MLA) for uniformity.
URLs in the bibliography lack access dates for some sources. Ensure each web reference includes an access date to strengthen academic rigor.
